text.title: Agentic Product Success Metrics
text.summary: Definitions and targets for goal achievement rate, autonomy ratio, and cost-per-task — the three primary metrics for agentic products.

rule.statement: Use goal achievement rate, autonomy ratio, and cost-per-task as primary agentic product metrics — not click-through rate or cost-per-user.
rule.rationale: Agentic systems scale with task volume, not seat count. Standard product metrics measure user engagement; agentic metrics measure the system's reliability and economic efficiency at accomplishing goals.

list.item: Goal achievement rate: fraction of tasks where the agent reached the defined goal state (autonomous + escalated where human approved).
Autonomy ratio: fraction of tasks completed without human intervention.
Failure rate: fraction of tasks that did not reach the goal state.
Cost-per-task: total inference + infrastructure cost divided by tasks completed.
Escalation rate: fraction of tasks that triggered a human-in-the-loop checkpoint.

rule.statement: Define "intelligence level" in concrete, testable terms before launch, not after.
rule.rationale: Vague intelligence claims ("the agent is smart") cannot be measured or improved. A testable definition ("the agent handles 80% of inbound support tickets without human review") enables sprint-over-sprint optimization.

example.note: "The agent handles 80% of inbound support tickets without human review, achieves goal achievement rate >= 0.90 across all ticket types, and costs < $0.05 per ticket."
"The agent will handle complex cases intelligently and escalate when uncertain."

rule.statement: Test goal achievement rate, not click-through rate. Require a 100-task pilot on real production data before committing an agentic feature to the roadmap.
rule.rationale: Agentic demos use cherry-picked inputs. Success in a controlled demo does not imply a viable goal achievement rate across real production traffic. The feedback loop is longer than for UI A/B tests.

text.reference: Sprint metrics dataclass for tracking autonomy ratio and goal achievement rate
